subject: Accounting
title: Petty Cash Management
description: A 60-second lesson on Petty Cash Management.

hook.text: An office needs $15 for stamps. Writing a check is overkill. That's what petty cash is for.

buildup.text: Petty cash is a small fund kept on hand for minor, everyday expenses.

discovery.text: A custodian manages the fund, keeping receipts for every disbursement to maintain accountability.

twist.text: Petty cash is a common fraud target—small amounts disappear and nobody notices for months.

climax.text: Regular surprise counts and strict documentation keep the fund honest.

punchline.text: Small money still needs big controls.

quiz.question: What is the main risk with petty cash?
options: Small amounts can disappear unnoticed without proper controls
